---
title: "Welcome to Mechanics of Materials"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
canvas:
  type: page
  published: true
---

![Course banner](../graphics/test_image.png)

Welcome to **MECH201 — Mechanics of Materials**. This course covers axial
loading, torsion, bending, and combined stresses in deformable bodies.

<img src="../graphics/test_image.png" alt="Inline HTML logo" width="160" />

## Core Relationships

Normal (axial) stress and strain are defined as

$$
\sigma = \frac{F}{A}, \qquad \varepsilon = \frac{\Delta L}{L_0}.
$$

Within the elastic region, Hooke's law links them through Young's modulus $E$:
the inline relation $\sigma = E\,\varepsilon$ holds until the proportional limit.

Strain energy stored in an axially loaded bar is

$$
U = \int_0^{L} \frac{N(x)^2}{2 E A}\, dx .
$$

## SI Notation

| Symbol | Quantity | SI Unit |
|:-------|:---------|:--------|
| $\sigma$ | Normal stress | $\text{Pa} = \text{N/m}^2$ |
| $\tau$ | Shear stress | $\text{Pa}$ |
| $\varepsilon$ | Normal strain | — (dimensionless) |
| $E$ | Young's modulus | $\text{GPa}$ |
| $I$ | Second moment of area | $\text{m}^4$ |

## What You Will Do

- Analyse statically determinate trusses
- Compute bending stress in beams
- Sit short concept quizzes

## Getting Started

Start with the [Truss Analysis assignment](../02_Statics/01_Truss_Analysis.qmd),
then take the [Syllabus Quiz](07_Syllabus_Quiz.qmd). The
[Beam Bending concept quiz](../03_Beam Bending/01_Concept_Quiz.qmd) comes later.

For unit conversions, see the [Engineering ToolBox](https://www.engineeringtoolbox.com/).
